title: enkleia thorelii
intro: Enkleia thorelii, a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae, is also known as the desert broom or silver bush. It is native to the deserts of Western Australia and is prized for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin irritations, and respiratory infections, and is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting immune function. Enkleia thorelii is commonly prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve, and is often used in traditional medicine and herbal remedies.
